chapter: Chapter 3: Electricity: Circuits and their Components

questions.question: Which combination of components is essential to make a bulb glow in a simple circuit?
questions.explanation: A simple circuit requires a power source (cell), a load (bulb), and connecting wires to complete the path.

questions.question: Why does a bulb not glow when the circuit is open?

options.C: Current cannot flow through a broken path

questions.explanation: In an open circuit, the path is broken, preventing electric current from flowing.

questions.question: In a series circuit, if one bulb fuses, what happens to the other bulbs?
questions.explanation: In a series circuit, all components share the same path. If one bulb fuses, the circuit breaks and current stops everywhere.

questions.question: Which property of a material determines whether it is a good conductor or insulator?
questions.explanation: Materials with low resistance are good conductors, while those with high resistance are insulators.

questions.question: Which instrument should be connected in series to measure current?
questions.explanation: An ammeter is connected in series so that the entire current flows through it for measurement.

questions.question: Why are household appliances connected in parallel circuits?
questions.explanation: Parallel circuits allow each appliance to receive the same voltage and operate independently.

questions.question: What is the main function of a fuse?
questions.explanation: A fuse melts when excess current flows, breaking the circuit and protecting devices.

questions.question: What happens to current when resistance increases while voltage remains constant?
questions.explanation: According to Ohm’s Law (I = V/R), increasing resistance reduces current when voltage is constant.

questions.question: Which of the following materials is a better conductor than iron?
questions.explanation: Copper has lower resistance and conducts electricity better than iron.

questions.question: Which symbol is used to represent a cell in a circuit diagram?

options.A: Two parallel lines of unequal length

questions.explanation: A cell is represented by two parallel lines of unequal length in circuit diagrams.

questions.question: Why are electrical wires coated with plastic?
questions.explanation: Plastic is an insulator and prevents accidental electric shocks.

questions.question: Which of the following causes a short circuit?

options.B: Direct contact between live and neutral wires

questions.explanation: When live and neutral wires come into direct contact, a short circuit occurs causing large current flow.

questions.question: What is the unit of electric current?
questions.explanation: Electric current is measured in amperes (A).

questions.question: Which component converts electrical energy into heat energy in a bulb?
questions.explanation: The filament heats up due to resistance and emits light.

questions.question: Which factor affects resistance of a wire?
questions.explanation: Resistance depends on the material, length, and thickness of the wire.

questions.question: Which safety device automatically switches off electricity during overload?
questions.explanation: MCB (Miniature Circuit Breaker) automatically trips during overload.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of parallel circuits?
questions.explanation: In parallel circuits, each device gets the same voltage and works independently.

questions.question: What does earthing help prevent?
questions.explanation: Earthing safely directs excess current into the ground, preventing shocks.

questions.question: If voltage increases and resistance remains constant, current will:
questions.explanation: From Ohm’s Law, increasing voltage increases current if resistance remains constant.

questions.question: Which of the following materials is commonly used as an insulator in wires?
questions.explanation: PVC (plastic) is used as insulation around electrical wires.

questions.question: Which condition will make a bulb glow brighter?
questions.explanation: Increasing voltage increases current, making the bulb glow brighter.

questions.question: Which diagram helps electricians understand connections in circuits?
questions.explanation: Circuit diagrams represent electrical connections using symbols.

questions.question: Which part of a bulb prevents filament from burning in air?

options.A: Glass bulb filled with inert gas

questions.explanation: The glass bulb contains inert gas which prevents filament from burning.

questions.question: Which of the following increases resistance in a wire?
questions.explanation: Longer wires have greater resistance.

questions.question: What happens when too many appliances are connected to one socket?
questions.explanation: Too many appliances draw excessive current, causing overloading.
